channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump signs order to gut staff at Voice of America and other US-funded media organizations
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration on Saturday began making deep cuts to Voice of America and other government-run, pro-democracy programming, with the organization's director saying all VOA employees have been put on leave.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI and DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Walz hits road to rip Trump policies as he works to keep his own national profile up
item.description: Gov. Tim Walz, the 2024 Democratic vice presidential nominee, held forums in Iowa and Nebraska. He is using them to dissect Trump administration actions and perhaps boost his own standing in the soul-searching Democratic Party.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: US strikes Houthis in Yemen as Trump warns Iran
item.description: President Donald Trump has ordered the United States military to "launch decisive and powerful" action against the Iran-backed Houthis in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Fetterman claps back at Ocasio-Cortez on government funding vote
item.description: Sen. John Fetterman (D-Pa.), who announced days ago that he would vote for a House-passed funding bill to avoid a government shutdown, said he doesn’t care about firebrand Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez’s (D-N.Y.) harsh criticism of Democratic senators who will vote to advance the controversial bill.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: House lawmakers hold hearing to further investigate Florida insurance companies
item.description: House lawmakers gathered at the statehouse Friday for a hearing to look into a bombshell report that Florida insurance companies were "ripping off" Floridians and misleading the legislature, claiming to be broke.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Appeals court lifts blocks on Trump's orders restricting diversity, equity and inclusion programs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An appeals court on Friday lifted a block on executive orders seeking to end government support for diversity, equity and inclusion programs, handing the Trump administration a win after a string of setbacks defending President Donald Trump’s agenda from dozens of lawsuits.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate unanimously approves fix to prevent $1B cuts for District of Columbia, sending bill to House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate swiftly approved legislation late Friday that would allow the District of Columbia to keep its budget intact, rather than roll back to 2024, fixing a provision that had drawn protests from the mayor and residents warning it would require $1 billion in cuts to services.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Most disapprove of Trump's approach to Ukraine, Russia: Survey
item.description: Nearly six in 10 people, 59 percent, said it was “not too" or “not at all likely” that Trump’s approach to the Russia-Ukraine war will bring long-term peace between the two Eastern European countries. Around 41 percent disagreed.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Canadian reporters grill Rubio over Trump's 51st state talk
item.description: Rubio said Trump’s stance on Canada joining the U.S. came following a dinner conversation with former Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Mark Carney is sworn in as Canada's new prime minister
item.description: Carney, a former Goldman Sachs executive with no experience in politics, becomes Canada's 24th prime minister.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 60 percent of voters unhappy with DOGE handling of federal workers: Poll
item.description: Elon Musk and DOGE have been trying to slash their way through the federal government, offering buyouts to and laying off scores of workers. Many of their actions are now the subject of lawsuits.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: White House withdraws nomination of David Weldon to run the CDC
item.description: Dr. Dave Weldon, Trump's pick for director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, was withdrawn from consideration shortly before a scheduled Senate confirmation hearing.
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang, MPR News

item.title: Nevada cuts wait time for inmates needing mental health care
item.description: The wait time for inmates needing a mental health bed to regain competency was down to an average 74 days compared to 78 when the 8 News Now Investigators reported on the statewide problem last fall.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump to increase steel and aluminum tariffs on Canada to 50 percent in response to electricity surcharge
item.description: The uncertainty around Trump's tariff plans have led to a downturn in the stock market, and the president has not ruled out the possibility of a recession this year.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Wall Street ends manic day modestly lower after briefly dropping more than 10% below its record
item.description: The U.S. stock market fell further Tuesday following President Donald Trump’s latest escalation in his trade war, briefly pulling Wall Street 10% below its record set last month. And like it's been most of the past few weeks, the market’s slide on Tuesday was erratic and dizzying.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Education Department says 60 universities under investigation for antisemitism
item.description: The Department of Education’s Office of Civil Rights (OCR) is investigating 60 universities for antisemitism, according to letters sent on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Florida bill would require some elementary school students to learn cursive
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — A new Florida bill would require some elementary school students to learn cursive writing. The bill, HB 921, would require all public schools to provide cursive writing instruction to students in grades 2 through 5.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Republicans worry Trump's tariffs could harm economy
item.description: Republican lawmakers are growing alarmed over signs that President Trump’s expanding trade war is hurting the economy, something they’re hearing from constituents at home who are struggling to adapt to Trump’s zigzagging tariff pronouncements.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about Mark Carney, elected next Canadian prime minister
item.description: Mark Carney, a former central banker, won his Liberal Party's election in a landslide on Sunday to replace Justin Trudeau as prime minister of Canada. He will be sworn into office in the coming days.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5
